seoTitle: How to Bulk Edit Issues in Jira Cloud Fast
metaDescription: Learn how to search, select, and update multiple Jira issues at once using the bulk change wizard, so you stop editing tickets one by one.
intro: Updating dozens of Jira tickets individually wastes time and creates inconsistent data across your boards. This guide shows how to use Jira's bulk change wizard to search for target issues, select them, and apply field updates across all of them in one pass.

steps.heading: Search or filter your target issues
steps.body: Use Jira's search or JQL filters to pull up the exact group of issues you want to modify before opening the bulk change tool.

steps.heading: Open the bulk change panel
steps.body: From the options menu, trigger the bulk change wizard, which lets you work across a batch of issues instead of editing them individually.

steps.heading: Select the issues to update
steps.body: Check the box to select all listed items, or choose specific cards while excluding any tickets you want to leave untouched.

steps.heading: Choose the operation and configure fields
steps.body: Pick the Edit Issues action, check the boxes for the fields you want to change — like assignee, labels, components, or status — and enter the new values.

steps.heading: Confirm and verify the update
steps.body: Review the summary of changes before confirming, then check your board afterward to make sure the updates applied correctly across all selected issues.

faq.question: How do I bulk edit issues in Jira?
faq.answer: Search for your target issues, open the options menu, choose Bulk Change All, select your issues, pick Edit Issues, and set the field values you want applied.

faq.question: Can I use JQL to select issues for a bulk edit?
faq.answer: Yes, using search filters or JQL queries lets you precisely group the issues you want before triggering the bulk change wizard.

faq.question: Will bulk editing affect issues across multiple boards?
faq.answer: Bulk changes apply to whatever issues you select in the search results, which can span multiple projects or boards depending on your filter.

faq.question: Is there a way to exclude specific tickets from a bulk edit?
faq.answer: Yes, instead of selecting all listed items you can manually check only the specific cards you want to include and leave others unselected.
